title: clusia criuva subsp. parviflora
intro: Clusia criuva subsp. parviflora, also known as the Clusia parviflora, is a plant species in the family Clusiaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Clusia criuva subsp. parviflora include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Clusia criuva subsp. parviflora can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
